exercises.title: Identify compensation for each saga step
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay checkout saga: 4 steps. What is the compensating transaction for each?
Step 1: ReserveInventory(itemId, qty)
Step 2: CreateOrder(merchantId, items)
Step 3: ChargePayment(merchantId, amount)
Step 4: SendConfirmationEmail(orderId, email)
exercises.expected_solution:
Step 1: ReserveInventory(itemId, qty)
   Compensation: ReleaseInventory(itemId, qty)
   Returns reserved stock to available pool
   Idempotent: if already released → no-op

Step 2: CreateOrder(merchantId, items)
   Compensation: CancelOrder(orderId)
   Mark order as CANCELLED (don't delete — keep for audit)
   Idempotent: if already cancelled → no-op

Step 3: ChargePayment(merchantId, amount)
   Compensation: RefundPayment(paymentId, amount)
   Issue a full refund to the customer
   Idempotent: check if already refunded before processing

Step 4: SendConfirmationEmail(orderId, email)
   IRREVERSIBLE: cannot un-send an email
   Compensation: none possible (email already delivered)
   Instead: send a follow-up 'order cancelled' email
   Alert: operations team if email sent for cancelled order
   NOTE: because step 4 is irreversible, ensure it's the LAST step
   (don't put irreversible actions in the middle of a saga)

test_cases.expected:
Step 1: ReserveInventory(itemId, qty)
   Compensation: ReleaseInventory(itemId, qty)
   Returns reserved stock to available pool
   Idempotent: if already released → no-op

Step 2: CreateOrder(merchantId, items)
   Compensation: CancelOrder(orderId)
   Mark order as CANCELLED (don't delete — keep for audit)
   Idempotent: if already cancelled → no-op

Step 3: ChargePayment(merchantId, amount)
   Compensation: RefundPayment(paymentId, amount)
   Issue a full refund to the customer
   Idempotent: check if already refunded before processing

Step 4: SendConfirmationEmail(orderId, email)
   IRREVERSIBLE: cannot un-send an email
   Compensation: none possible (email already delivered)
   Instead: send a follow-up 'order cancelled' email
   Alert: operations team if email sent for cancelled order
   NOTE: because step 4 is irreversible, ensure it's the LAST step
   (don't put irreversible actions in the middle of a saga)

hints: Compensation = undo of the forward step
Idempotent: calling compensation twice → same result as once
Irreversible: put last in saga; compensate with notification instead

exercises.title: Complete the saga orchestrator structure
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks in the saga orchestrator.
hints: state.mark(): track completed steps for compensation logic
compensate in reverse order: PAYMENT then INVENTORY
catch all exceptions: any step failure triggers compensation

exercises.title: SagaState tracker
exercises.instructions:
Write SagaState:
1. enum SagaStep: INVENTORY_RESERVED, ORDER_CREATED, PAYMENT_CHARGED, NOTIFICATION_SENT
2. SagaState: mark(step), is(step), getCompletedSteps()
3. @SpringBootTest: mark steps, check state, get completed list
hints: LinkedHashSet: maintains insertion order (important for compensation order)
is(step): O(1) lookup — Set.contains()
Mark BEFORE executing step? No — mark AFTER success (so we know to compensate)

exercises.title: Idempotent compensating transactions
exercises.instructions:
Write idempotent compensation methods:
1. InventoryService.release(itemId, qty): if already released → no-op
2. PaymentService.refund(paymentId): if already refunded → no-op
3. Both return a status indicating what happened
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): double-release → no-op, double-refund → no-op
hints: Set<String>: track processed IDs (in production: DB table with idempotency key)
Return result enum: caller knows if it was new or duplicate
ALREADY_RELEASED: return success (not error) — the end state is correct

exercises.title: Orchestration saga — full checkout flow
exercises.instructions:
Write CheckoutSagaOrchestrator:
1. 3 steps: reserve inventory, create order, charge payment
2. SagaState tracks each step
3. Any failure: compensate in reverse order
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): success→all steps, step2 fails→compensation, step3 fails→compensation
hints: Compensation in reverse: PAYMENT → ORDER → INVENTORY (reverse of forward)
state.is(step): check before compensating (don't compensate a step that never ran)
String.contains('FAIL2'): simple test hook to trigger failure at specific step

exercises.title: Choreography saga with Spring Events
exercises.instructions:
Implement choreography saga using Spring ApplicationEvents:
1. OrderService: create order → publish OrderCreatedEvent
2. InventoryService: @EventListener OrderCreatedEvent → reserve → publish InventoryReservedEvent
3. PaymentService: @EventListener InventoryReservedEvent → charge
4. @SpringBootTest: one event triggers the full chain
hints: Choreography: each service reacts to events without a central coordinator
Event chain: OrderCreated → InventoryReserved → PaymentCharged (automatic)
Downside: flow is implicit — hard to visualize the complete saga

exercises.title: Saga log for crash recovery
exercises.instructions:
Write SagaLog for crash recovery:
1. SagaLogEntry: sagaId, stepName, status (STARTED/COMPLETED/COMPENSATED), timestamp
2. SagaLogRepository: persist log to DB (JPA)
3. Orchestrator: log STARTED before each step, COMPLETED after, COMPENSATED on undo
4. @DataJpaTest: saga log recovers correct state
hints: STARTED before step: if crash, log shows where to resume/compensate
COMPLETED after step: saga can continue past this step after restart
findBySagaIdAndStepName: check if a specific step reached COMPLETED state

exercises.title: Choreography saga failure and compensation
exercises.instructions:
Add failure handling to choreography saga:
1. PaymentFailedEvent: published when payment fails
2. InventoryService: @EventListener PaymentFailedEvent → release inventory
3. OrderService: @EventListener PaymentFailedEvent → cancel order
4. @SpringBootTest: PaymentFailed → both compensations run
hints: PaymentFailedEvent: broadcast — all services that need to compensate subscribe
Choreography downside: hard to ensure all compensation listeners are in place
No central coordinator: each service decides its own compensation logic

exercises.title: Saga state machine with status tracking
exercises.instructions:
Build a saga state machine:
1. SagaStatus enum: CREATED, INVENTORY_RESERVED, ORDER_CREATED, PAYMENT_CHARGED, COMPLETED, COMPENSATING, COMPENSATED, FAILED
2. SagaStateMachine: allowed transitions (no skipping, no backward without COMPENSATING)
3. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): valid transitions, invalid transition throws, compensation path
hints: State machine: prevent invalid saga states (skip steps, wrong order)
COMPENSATING: intermediate state when undo is in progress
Map.of: immutable allowed transitions (clean declarative style)

exercises.title: Saga failure analysis
exercises.instructions:
5-step saga: Inventory → Order → Payment → Warehouse → Email.
Step 3 (Payment) fails AFTER steps 1 and 2 succeeded.
Step 4 (Warehouse) NEVER ran.

1. Which steps need compensation?
2. In what order?
3. What if the compensation for step 2 (OrderCancel) also fails?
4. What if the email service is never reached but crashes?
5. Can we use 2PC instead for this scenario?
exercises.expected_solution:
1. Steps needing compensation:
   Step 2 (Order) → needs CancelOrder compensation
   Step 1 (Inventory) → needs ReleaseInventory compensation
   Step 3 (Payment) → FAILED, nothing to compensate (nothing was done)
   Step 4 (Warehouse) → NEVER RAN, nothing to compensate
   Step 5 (Email) → NEVER RAN, nothing to compensate

2. Compensation order (reverse of forward):
   First: CancelOrder (undo step 2)
   Then: ReleaseInventory (undo step 1)
   Always reverse — dependencies require it
   (Order was created AFTER inventory → cancel order BEFORE releasing inventory)

3. OrderCancel compensation ALSO fails:
   Retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, max 30s delay)
   If all retries fail: SAGA STUCK (manual intervention needed)
   Alert: ops team with saga ID + failed step
   Dead letter queue: saga failure event for async processing
   'Saga of sagas': a monitoring saga watches for stuck sagas

4. Email service crashes:
   Email never ran → no compensation needed
   But saga needs to handle: retry email? alert? ignore?
   Policy decision: email is 'best-effort' → saga marks COMPLETED_WITH_WARNING

5. 2PC instead:
   2PC requires all services to lock resources during the transaction:
   Inventory DB locked → Order DB locked → Payment gateway locked
   Network call to Payment gateway during lock: payment gateways don't support 2PC!
   Lock duration: entire saga time (seconds to minutes) → huge contention
   Node failure during 2PC: system stuck waiting for recovery
   Verdict: 2PC impossible for cross-system sagas (payment gateways, external APIs)

test_cases.expected:
1. Steps needing compensation:
   Step 2 (Order) → needs CancelOrder compensation
   Step 1 (Inventory) → needs ReleaseInventory compensation
   Step 3 (Payment) → FAILED, nothing to compensate (nothing was done)
   Step 4 (Warehouse) → NEVER RAN, nothing to compensate
   Step 5 (Email) → NEVER RAN, nothing to compensate

2. Compensation order (reverse of forward):
   First: CancelOrder (undo step 2)
   Then: ReleaseInventory (undo step 1)
   Always reverse — dependencies require it
   (Order was created AFTER inventory → cancel order BEFORE releasing inventory)

3. OrderCancel compensation ALSO fails:
   Retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, max 30s delay)
   If all retries fail: SAGA STUCK (manual intervention needed)
   Alert: ops team with saga ID + failed step
   Dead letter queue: saga failure event for async processing
   'Saga of sagas': a monitoring saga watches for stuck sagas

4. Email service crashes:
   Email never ran → no compensation needed
   But saga needs to handle: retry email? alert? ignore?
   Policy decision: email is 'best-effort' → saga marks COMPLETED_WITH_WARNING

5. 2PC instead:
   2PC requires all services to lock resources during the transaction:
   Inventory DB locked → Order DB locked → Payment gateway locked
   Network call to Payment gateway during lock: payment gateways don't support 2PC!
   Lock duration: entire saga time (seconds to minutes) → huge contention
   Node failure during 2PC: system stuck waiting for recovery
   Verdict: 2PC impossible for cross-system sagas (payment gateways, external APIs)

hints: Only compensate COMPLETED steps (not the failing step itself)
2PC: distributed locks across external systems = impractical
Compensation failure: retry + dead letter queue + alert

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay checkout saga
exercises.instructions:
Build complete checkout saga:
1. 3-step orchestration: inventory reserve → order create → payment charge
2. Idempotent compensations for each
3. Saga log (JPA entity) persists state
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): success, step2 fail + compensation, step3 fail + compensation
5. All 3 pass
hints: SagaLog: save STARTED before action, DONE after (for crash recovery)
failAt=2: triggers failure at step 2 in test (simple but effective)
Compensation: set value to false (reversed from true=active)

exercises.title: Saga with external payment gateway (at-most-once vs at-least-once)
exercises.instructions:
Handle payment gateway idempotency:
1. Payment gateway uses idempotency key: charge(paymentId, idempKey)
2. If saga retries: same idempKey → gateway deduplicates
3. RefundService: check if already refunded before refund
4. @SpringBootTest: retry same idempKey → gateway processes once
hints: Idempotency key: client-generated UUID per payment attempt
Same key on retry: return original result (not process again)
Payment at-least-once + idempotency: retry safe without double charge

exercises.title: Complete saga with Kafka-based choreography
exercises.instructions:
Design Kafka-based choreography saga:
1. OrderService: publishEvent(OrderCreatedEvent) to 'orders' Kafka topic
2. InventoryService: @KafkaListener 'orders' → reserve → publish to 'inventory'
3. CompensationConsumer: listens to 'payments.failed' → triggers compensations
4. @SpringBootTest with mock Kafka: verify event flow
hints: @MockBean KafkaTemplate: avoids real Kafka in tests
Choreography: services react to events, no central coordinator
Compensation consumer: separate @KafkaListener for failure events

exercises.title: Saga monitoring with Micrometer
exercises.instructions:
Add monitoring to saga execution:
1. Counter: saga.completed (tag: outcome={success,compensated})
2. Timer: saga.duration (measure total saga execution time)
3. Counter: saga.compensation.triggered (tag: step)
4. @SpringBootTest: run sagas → verify metrics
hints: Timer.start() + timer.stop(): measures saga duration
Counter with outcome tag: distinguish success vs compensated sagas
saga.compensation.triggered: alert when compensation rate is high

exercises.title: Complete M24 T3 Saga synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M24 T3 synthesis:
1. 3-step orchestration saga (inventory, order, payment) with state tracking
2. Idempotent compensations (Set-based check)
3. Saga state machine: valid transitions only
4. Micrometer: saga.completed counter
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): success, step2 fail, compensation idempotent, metric correct
6. All 4 pass
hints: ALREADY_DONE: idempotent compensation returns different result on second call (but succeeds)
released Set: check before releasing — idempotency check
Counter count()>=1: not ==1 because other tests may also increment
